column2:
Domain = namespace subtree; zone = administratively served portion bounded by delegation cuts.
Why: the unit of authority/transfer/signing.
Trap: assuming every subdomain is a zone.

Stub (asks, caches), recursive (iterates, caches), forwarder (delegates recursion upstream, caches), authoritative (serves zone data, AA).
Why: every ticket starts by naming the role.
Trap: "the DNS server" without a role.

Empty ANSWER; AUTHORITY = child NS; ADDITIONAL = glue; no AA.
Why: the mechanism of iteration.
Trap: glue is hint data, not parent authority.

NS names in-bailiwick (inside the delegated zone).
Why: circular dependency otherwise.
Trap: out-of- bailiwick NS removes the need entirely.

Name absent vs type absent (NOERROR/0 + SOA).
Why: different causes, both negative-cached.
Trap: empty non-terminals answer NODATA, never NXDOMAIN.

min(SOA MINIMUM, SOA TTL) from the moment cached, per cache.
Why: create-after-query latency.
Trap: record TTL is irrelevant to negatives.

TC=1 retry; AXFR/IXFR; policy/ DoT.
Why: "UDP-only firewall" = intermittent size-dependent failures.
Trap: "TCP only for transfers."

Fits min-MTU paths unfragmented — silent fragment loss becomes clean TC→TCP.
Why: modern transport posture.
Trap: the CLIENT advertises the size.

NAT, new subnets, VPN pools, ACL order.
Why: view selection = source IP + first match.
Trap: "data changed" when it differs by vantage.

column1: [C10] forward only vs first under dead upstream?
column2:
only→SERVFAIL; first→own iteration IF hints/egress work.
Why: untested fallback is theater.
Trap: validate the fallback in lab.

L1 objects (intent), L2 deployed zone data, L3 caches.
Why: every finding names its layer.
Trap: blaming L3 (TTL) for L1/L2 gaps.

App → OS stub → forwarder → resolver node(s) → negatives within each.
Why: staleness enumeration.
Trap: the forgotten forwarder layer.

Any exact name (any type) stops wildcard synthesis for ALL types at that name.
Why: a TXT add can kill A resolution.
Trap: T10.

CNAME must be alone at its node (DNSSEC metadata excepted); never at apex.
Why: §4 case 3 refusals.
Trap: apex "ALIAS" = provider flattening, not CNAME.

column1: [C15] What does DNSSEC provide / not provide?
column2:
Authenticity + integrity; never confidentiality.
Why: DoT/DoH is the privacy tool.
Trap: "DNSSEC encrypts."

Validated (AD); provably unsigned (signed DS absence — resolves fine); should-validate-but-fails (SERVFAIL).
Why: unsigned ≠ broken.
Trap: the middle state exists by design.

column1: [C17] Where does DS live and what does it digest?
column2:
Parent zone; child's KSK.
Why: the one record you don't control.
Trap: stale DS after KSK roll = T30.

Per-instance caches diverge behind one IP.
Why: T28; monitoring must probe instances.
Trap: flushing "the resolver" = flushing one instance.

Clients on other resolvers/DoH; direct-to-IP traffic.
Why: honest scoping to security teams.
Trap: overselling.

No caching — every lookup rides to authority.
Why: load + inherited authority availability per query.
Trap: not "agile best practice." ## B. FLAGS & RCODES

An authoritative server, from zone data.
Why: source truth.
Trap: AA proves source, not correctness.

column1: [F02] qr rd ra, no aa, TTL draining?
column2:
Recursive resolver, cache.
Why: provenance in one glance.
Trap: draining across repeats to the SAME node.

Echo of the query — proves nothing about recursion happening.
Trap: the echo.

AD: responder validated DNSSEC; CD: querier says skip validation.
Why: +cd is the DNSSEC bisector.
Trap: AD ≠ "authoritative."

Retry same query over TCP.
Why: normal protocol, only the failed TCP retry is an incident.
Trap: treating TC as an error.

The path/zone: upstream dead, broken delegation, expired zone, DNSSEC bogus — bisect per hop, +cd early.
Trap: chains launder REFUSED into SERVFAIL.

Policy at THAT responder (ACL/view/role).
Why: read it at the hop that emitted it.
Trap: REFUSED ≠ down (TCP conversation happened).

Transport — nothing DNS-level returned.
Why: capture at server = cheapest bisect.
Trap: ACLs REFUSE, they don't drop.

Cache X-ray — answers only from cache.
Why: staleness/liveness probe without polluting.
Trap: per-node on farms.

NODATA.
Why: the SOA is the negative-cache timer.
Trap: not an error. ## C. RECORDS

RFC 1982 arithmetic — secondaries act only on increase; backwards = frozen.
Why: replication protocol in one number.
Trap: recovery = retransfer or +2^31 wrap.

How long secondaries serve without successful refresh, then SERVFAIL.
Why: your real DR deadline (T23).
Trap: staleness invisible in flags until the cliff.

Negative-cache TTL (RFC 2308).
Why: create-latency physics.
Trap: historic "default TTL" meaning is dead.

Lowest preference first; target = hostname with A/AAAA — no CNAME, no IP literal.
Trap: the CNAME target.

priority weight port target (at _service._proto.name).
Why: AD lives on these.
Trap: weight only among equal priorities.

255-byte strings, multiple quoted segments concatenated by consumers.
Why: DKIM truncation incidents.
Trap: exactly ONE v=spf1 per name.

15.20.10.10.in-addr.arpa.
Why: octet reversal reflex.
Trap: nothing links PTR↔A except process.

Which CAs may issue certs; walked up labels at issuance.
Why: "cert renewal failed" tickets.
Trap: absent CAA = any CA.

Whole RRset cached per TTL; rotation at serving side; balance averages across clients.
Trap: "duplicate" deletion = capacity surgery (T06).

Dotless target gets $ORIGIN appended → name.zone.zone NXDOMAIN.
Why: the classic zone-file hour-burner.
Trap: hides in CNAME/MX/SRV targets. ## D. BIND OPERATIONS

edit→checkzone→serial++→reload→ NOTIFY→SOA check→IXFR/AXFR→converged→caches drain.
Why: name the failure at each arrow.
Trap: NOTIFY is a hint; REFRESH is the guarantee.

Delta history (journal; static zones need ixfr-from-differences); else clean AXFR fallback.
Trap: fallback is correct behavior, not an error.

BADSIG=secret, BADTIME=clock, BADKEY=name, plain REFUSED=unsigned vs key ACL.
Why: four different fixes.
Trap: NTP is a DNS dependency.

Flush journal→file, suspend updates, edit, resume.
Why: dynamic-zone truth = journal+memory (T21).
Trap: hand-edit without it = desync.

reload re-reads zones; reconfig = config + NEW zones only.
Why: busy-server safety.

named-checkconf -z (config + every zone load).
Why: triage step 2, always.
Trap: passes ≠ semantically right data.

Loaded/serial/type/refresh timers/expiry countdown.
Why: THE secondary-health command.
Trap: check before any surgery.

status → checkconf -z → zonestatus → logs → dig local (right view) → dig remote → surgery last.
Why: evidence before change.
Trap: dig-and-flush reflex.

Whole-zone grant vs per-key/name/type least privilege.
Why: AD-style self-updates pattern.
Trap: unkeyed allow-update = anyone writes.

Periodic TC replies — real clients retry TCP, spoofed victims get nothing.
Why: amplification defense that spares users.

column1: [B11] Query log flags +E T D S?
column2:
RD, EDNS, TCP, DO, TSIG-signed.
Why: read the line, know the client.

Grep N-day query logs for the name; hits = alive, find sources.
Why: prevents T-class deletion incidents.
Trap: external verification TXTs never show liveness. ## E. EFFICIENTIP / DDI

Exact dup / same-name- diff-value / type conflict / automation collision.
Why: four different actions.
Trap: one generic response.

Template materializing one logical zone as wired primary/secondaries across members.
Why: it's BIND underneath — Chapter 4 skills apply.
Trap: hand edits get overwritten.

Reverse zone not under platform management → forward-only creates.
Why: drift class #2.
Trap: "IPAM keeps it consistent" — only for managed paths.

Staged change, failed push, unreachable agent, hand-edit divergence.
Why: T20/T47.
Trap: the TTL reflex.

Mapping preview, on-error semantics, dry-run existence, round-trip export.
Why: T32 prevention.
Trap: skip-and-continue + shifted columns.

Name-only can hit round-robin members or wrong record.
Why: T50 landmines.

Captured BEFORE apply, re-applicable form, covers all touched objects, handles half-applied.
Trap: nightly backup ≠ rollback artifact.

Direct edits only with mandatory reconciliation before next deploy.
Why: T47's second outage.
Trap: "faster on the server." ## F. TROUBLESHOOTING DECISION POINTS

Provenance ladder: client → each resolver node → each auth → parent.
Why: deepest wrong layer owns it.
Trap: stopping at first plausible cause (T49).

tcpdump at the server: did anything arrive?.
Why: strongest bisect in networking (T25).
Trap: theorizing past an empty capture.

One stale node of four behind VIP/anycast.
Why: T19/T28 fingerprint.
Trap: diagnosing through the VIP.

A corpse in a list (dead forwarder/NS) burning a timeout.
Why: T15 fingerprint.
Trap: "internet is slow."

Transport: EDNS/fragmentation/TCP blocked — bufsize matrix + +tcp.
Why: T16/T48.
Trap: "record corrupt."

DNSSEC validation class → delv → DS/DNSKEY/RRSIG chain.
Why: three-query triage.
Trap: only-my-resolver = my anchor/clock/path.

Client-side cache — including Windows NEGATIVE cache.
Why: Clear-DnsClientCache.
Trap: declaring done at resolver level.

hosts file / nsswitch / app cache / pinned IP — getent is the app's-eye view.
Why: exoneration standard (T04).
Trap: "not DNS" without naming the culprit.

Cache state — dumpdb + timestamped digs BEFORE any flush.
Why: flush destroys the crime scene.
Trap: surgery first.

Never — state convergence as clock time + negative-TTL tail.
Why: TTL honesty = the senior sentence.
Trap: the word "propagation" as weather. ## G. COMMANDS (drill until reflex)

dig @auth name → aa + full TTL every repeat.
Trap: AA ≠ correct data.

No aa + TTL draining across repeats (same node); +norecurse still answers.
Trap: farm nodes differ.
SOA TTL in AUTHORITY of the NXDOMAIN/NODATA answer, draining.

rndc flushname (tree for subtrees; full flush = storm).
Trap: per node.
Get-DnsClientCache / Clear-DnsClientCache (ipconfig /displaydns /flushdns) — negatives show as "Name does not exist.".

getent hosts name (NSS truth); dig bypasses NSS.
Trap: 127.0.0.53 = resolved's view.

delv — "fully validated" vs failing link named.
Trap: dig +cd first to classify.

dig @primary zone AXFR (TSIG: -y type:name:secret).
Why: also the security audit.
Trap: unauth success = finding.

dig SOA @each-auth +short, compare.
Why: free monitoring, catches T12.
Trap: equality necessary, not sufficient post-incident (T44).

dig CH TXT id.server (or hostname.bind) @IP.
Why: T28.
